communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Danley God Squad SOL
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Any written or electronic communications, including emails, texts, Microsoft Teams messages, Signal chats, or DMs, sent or received by Christopher Danley that reference the Endangered Species Committee, also known as the "God Squad". This include any references to the committee as it relates to Doug Burgum's decision in March 2026 to convene said committee. This request also includes any communications between Danley and White House or Defense Department personnel concerning that reference the Endangered Species Committee explicitly or implicitly. I seek records produced since January 1, 2026.

I am a reporter for Public Domain and seek a fee waiver. If that waiver is denied I am willing to pay $25.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jimmy Tobias

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Danley Arena
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Any and all written or electronic communications, including emails, texts, Team meetings, Signal messages and DMs, sent or received by Christopher Danley that reference Arena Offshore, an offshore oil developer in the Gulf of America. This request seeks records since June 1, 2025. 

I am a reporter for Public Domain and seek a fee waiver. IF that waiver is denied I am willing to pay $25.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jimmy Tobias

communications.subject: New York Freedom of Information Law Request: AER - NY Department of Health
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New York Freedom of Information Law, I hereby request the following records:

All adverse medical event and sentinel event records filed with the New York Department of Health between Jan. 1, 2023 and Jan. 1, 2025.

I request these records in their native digital formats and not in formats that degrade the accessibility of the records, such as tabular data converted to PDF or Word files. If converting the native digital format to another high-fidelity format, such as CSV or Excel, please take care not to introduce formatting inconsistencies or errors, which can occur in the conversion process (for example, encoding errors or the inclusion of internal unescaped quotation marks in CSV files). Should the records above represent certain values as numeric codes or abbreviations that are not self-explanatory, we additionally request all records necessary to interpret those codes and abbreviations.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Michael Nolan

communications.subject: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request: Standard Records Request — Baltimore
communications.communication:
April 3, 2026

Re: Public Records Request — Holiday Lighting Procurement Records

Dear Xavier A. Conaway,

Pursuant to the Maryland Public Information Act (PIA) (Md. Code, GP § 4-101), I am writing to request access to and copies of the following public records from Baltimore:

all records related to holiday lighting and seasonal decorative lighting services, including but not limited to: contracts, purchase orders, invoices, bid submissions, vendor proposals, requests for proposals (RFPs), requests for qualifications (RFQs), scope of work documents, vendor selection criteria, evaluation scoring sheets, and any correspondence with vendors or contractors related to holiday or seasonal lighting services.

This request covers the past five (5) fiscal years.

As you may be aware, the Maryland Public Information Act (PIA) requires a response within 10 days of receipt of this request.

I would prefer to receive these records in electronic format (PDF, Excel, or other digital format) delivered via email to rfp@britenites.com. If electronic delivery is not available, please advise on alternative arrangements.

Regarding fees: I request a fee waiver as this information is being sought in the public interest. If a fee waiver is not available, please provide a cost estimate before proceeding with the request if anticipated fees will exceed $25.00.

If any portion of this request is denied, please cite the specific statutory exemption and provide any non-exempt portions that can be reasonably segregated.

Thank you for your prompt attention to this request. Please feel free to contact me at rfp@britenites.com if you have any questions or need clarification.

Sincerely,
Dillon Williams
rfp@britenites.com
